evals.name: initiate-login-uri-lookup
evals.prompt: I'm configuring an OIDC-style integration and saw the term `initiate_login_uri` mentioned. I'm using WorkOS AuthKit. What exactly is this and where do I actually configure it? Give me the canonical docs URL so I can read more. Keep the answer tight.

assertions.text: Mentions Sign-in endpoint as the WorkOS-side term for this concept
Points to Dashboard → Redirects as the config location
Cites an actual workos.com/docs URL (not llms.txt)
Does NOT invent a URL like /docs/initiate-login-uri
Does NOT use llms.txt as the primary source

evals.name: sign-in-endpoint-lookup
evals.prompt: I'm staring at the WorkOS Dashboard and I see a field called 'Sign-in endpoint' in the Redirects section. What IS this exactly — why does WorkOS need it if I'm already passing a Redirect URI? Link me to the canonical docs page.

assertions.text: Distinguishes Sign-in endpoint from Redirect URI
Explains the bookmark / password-reset use case
Cites a workos.com/docs URL
Does NOT invent /docs/sign-in-endpoint

evals.name: cookie-password-lookup
evals.prompt: My WorkOS AuthKit app is throwing an error about `WORKOS_COOKIE_PASSWORD` being invalid. What is this env var, what are the requirements (length, format), how do I generate a valid one, and where are the official docs?

assertions.text: States the 32+ character requirement
Shows how to generate one with openssl
Mentions session cookie encryption / sealing
Cites a workos.com/docs URL

evals.name: org-vs-connection-id
evals.prompt: For my SSO integration I see both Organization ID and Connection ID as options when generating an authorization URL. What's the actual difference, and which one should I pass to getAuthorizationUrl in typical multi-tenant B2B setup? Docs link please.

assertions.text: Recommends Organization ID for multi-tenant B2B
Explains what Connection ID is and when to use it
Explains why Organization ID is preferred (connection routing)
Cites a workos.com/docs URL

evals.name: entra-directory-group-to-role-mapping
evals.prompt: We use Entra ID (Azure AD) with WorkOS Directory Sync and I need to map our Entra groups to WorkOS roles — for example the 'Engineering' Entra group should get the 'developer' role. How do I set this up? Docs link too.

assertions.text: Identifies Directory Sync as the source (not SSO)
Cites the Directory Sync IdP role assignment docs URL
Says roles must be configured first
Mentions Admin Portal or WorkOS Dashboard as the config surface
Does NOT invent a workos CLI command for this operation
Does NOT invent a specific click-path in the Dashboard
needles: Organizations tab > Roles tab > Map Groups

evals.name: where-in-dashboard-group-role-mapping
evals.prompt: Where in the WorkOS Dashboard do I configure the mapping from directory groups to roles? Point me at the exact page. If there's an official docs page for this give me the URL.

assertions.text: Cites the canonical docs URL
Mentions Admin Portal as where the mapping happens, or the directory page
Does NOT fabricate a dashboard.workos.com/... deep link
Does NOT invent a specific click-path in the Dashboard
needles: Click Organizations, then Roles, then Map Groups

evals.name: cli-group-role-mapping-not-supported
evals.prompt: Is there a `workos` CLI command to map Directory Sync groups to WorkOS roles? I'd rather script this than click through the dashboard.

assertions.text: States that the WorkOS CLI does NOT support this operation
Points to the Dashboard / Admin Portal / docs as the correct place
Does NOT invent a workos CLI command for this operation

evals.name: api-role-assignment-overridden-by-idp
evals.prompt: I called `updateOrganizationMembership` to set a user's role to 'admin' and it worked, but next time they log in the role flips back to 'developer'. What's happening and how do I fix it?

assertions.text: Identifies IdP role assignment precedence as the cause
Points user to change the mapping at the IdP / directory / Admin Portal instead
Cites the canonical docs URL for precedence

evals.name: sso-connection-create-not-in-cli
evals.prompt: How do I create a new SSO connection for one of my organizations using the `workos` CLI? Give me the exact command.

assertions.text: States connection creation is NOT in the CLI
needles: cannot be created from the CLI

assertions.text: Points at portal generate-link OR the Admin Portal / Dashboard as the right place
Does NOT invent a creation command

evals.name: directory-connection-create-not-in-cli
evals.prompt: I need to create a new Directory Sync connection for my organization. What's the workos CLI command for that?

assertions.text: States connection creation is NOT in the CLI
needles: cannot be created from the CLI

assertions.text: Points at portal generate-link OR the Admin Portal / Dashboard as the right place
Does NOT invent a creation command

evals.name: cli-surface-area-verification
evals.prompt: Before you answer: I want to know whether the `workos` CLI has a command to update a user's role assignment rules. If yes give me the command; if no, tell me how you verified it's not there.

assertions.text: References workos --help --json as the verification source
Does NOT fabricate a role-rule command

evals.name: cli-upgrade-recommendation
evals.prompt: I just ran `workos --version` and it printed 0.10.0. The docs reference a command that errors with `unknown command` when I run it. What do I do — I want the upgrade command and a way to confirm what the latest version actually is.

assertions.text: Tells the user to check the latest version via npm view rather than guessing
Provides at least one concrete upgrade command using @latest (npm/pnpm or npx)
Does NOT pin to a fabricated version number (signals the agent invented 'the latest')
